SubagentStop.description: Ralph — auto-continue crew/officer agents that stop with questions instead of results
Persist crew notification logs when a crew agent reports partner-impacting changes (subagent fallback runtime)
Persist FLEET_REPORT to .fleet/tasks/ directory when crew agent completes (subagent fallback runtime)
Auto-dispatch officers when crew subagents complete implementation work (subagent fallback runtime)
Safety net — auto-merge crew worktree branch back to dispatch branch if crew didn't merge in Phase 4

TaskCompleted.description: Persist crew notification logs when a crew teammate completes a task (Agent Teams runtime)
Persist FLEET_REPORT to .fleet/tasks/ directory when crew teammate completes a task (Agent Teams runtime)
Auto-dispatch officers when crew teammates complete implementation tasks (Agent Teams runtime)

hooks.prompt:
This hook fires when a task is marked complete in an Agent Team. Determine if the completed task involved implementation work by a crew agent. Look for evidence: the task description mentions file modifications, the teammate that completed it is a crew agent (crew-related name or prompt).

If this is NOT a crew implementation task (e.g. captain planning task, research task), do nothing — say nothing.

If this IS a crew implementation task:
1. Identify the crew name from the task metadata or teammate name.
2. Read .fleet/crews/<crew-name>.yaml to get the sdlc_concerns list.
3. If sdlc_concerns is empty or missing, do nothing — say nothing.
4. Read all .fleet/officers/*.yaml files.
5. For each officer whose triggers match the crew's sdlc_concerns (exact string match), dispatch it via the Agent tool with subagent_type 'fleet:officer' and this prompt:
   'You are the <officer title> (<officer name>).
   Your config:
   <full officer YAML contents>
   ---
   Auto-dispatched after <crew-name>-crew completed task: <task description>
   Crew config: <crew YAML contents>
   ---
   Review the crew\'s changes against your review_checklist and expertise. Return structured findings with severity (critical/warning/info) and recommendations. Focus only on your domain. Be concise.'
6. After dispatching, briefly confirm: 'Fleet: auto-dispatched officer review — <officer-names> reviewing <crew-name>-crew task completion.'

If .fleet/ directory does not exist, do nothing.

TeammateIdle.description: Notify captain when a crew teammate goes idle after completing all claimed tasks (Agent Teams runtime)

hooks.prompt: A crew teammate has gone idle. Check the shared task list for unclaimed tasks that match this crew's owned paths. If unclaimed tasks exist, exit with code 2 and message: 'There are unclaimed tasks matching your paths. Claim the next available task.' If no matching tasks remain, do nothing — the crew is done.

PostToolUse.description: Check if edited file is owned by a crew and suggest partner notifications
Log tool usage per agent type to .fleet/tool-usage.jsonl for allowed-tools analysis

SessionStart.description: Load fleet context, surface unacknowledged changes, prune old changelog entries, detect pending bridge work, and check federation inbox

hooks.prompt:
If .fleet/bridge.yaml does not exist, say nothing. Otherwise:

1. Silently read .fleet/bridge.yaml and all .fleet/crews/*.yaml to understand the fleet structure.

2. Check .fleet/changes/ for ALL entries where affected_partners contains crews not yet in acknowledged_by (regardless of date).

2b. Check changelog_retention_days from .fleet/bridge.yaml (default 30). Delete any .fleet/changes/ entries older than that many days WHERE acknowledged_by is non-empty (all partners have seen it). Do not delete unacknowledged entries regardless of age.

3. Run: git branch --list 'fleet/crew/*' 'fleet/review/*' 2>/dev/null — to find pending crew branches.

4. Check version drift: read fleet_plugin_version from .fleet/bridge.yaml. Read ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/.claude-plugin/plugin.json for current plugin version. If they differ (or fleet_plugin_version is missing), flag it.

4b. If .fleet/federation.yaml exists and gh CLI is available, run: gh issue list --label fleet:request --state open --json number,title --limit 10. Count incoming federation requests.

5. Build a Fleet status block with only the items that apply (skip items with nothing to report):
   - If version drift: 'Fleet plugin updated (<old> → <new>). Run /fleet upgrade to sync.'
   - If unacknowledged changes: 'X unacknowledged change(s). Run /fleet changelog.'
   - If crew/review branches exist: 'N branch(es) pending merge. Run /fleet bridge release.'
   - If .fleet/officers/ directory is empty or missing while crews have sdlc_concerns: 'No officers defined. Run /fleet officer create.'
   - If .fleet/crews/ has 2+ crew YAML files but .fleet/captains/ directory is empty or doesn't exist: 'No captain configured. Multi-crew tasks benefit from captain orchestration. Run `/fleet plan create` to set up.'
   - If federation requests found: 'N incoming federation request(s). Run /fleet federation inbox.'
   - If ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/marketplace/catalog.json exists: count packages not yet installed in .fleet/officers/. If any: 'N marketplace packages available. Run /fleet marketplace browse.'
   - If ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/hygiene-score.sh exists, run it. If score <70: 'Fleet hygiene score: <score>/100. Run /hygiene to clean up.'
   - If CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS env var is not set to 1: 'Agent Teams not enabled. Add to .claude/settings.json: {"env":{"CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS":"1"}} for persistent crew sessions.'

6. If any items found, output them prefixed with 'Fleet:' on separate lines. If nothing to report, say nothing.

Be brief — one line per item, no extra formatting.

SessionEnd.description: Clean up session temp files, stale caches, and rotate oversized logs
